Navigation: ParaBank is a sample banking application provided by Parasoft for testing purposes. The main layout consists of a left sidebar and a main content area. The left sidebar is divided into two sections: at the top, the Customer Login panel displays Username and Password fields with a "Log In" button, along with "Forgot login info?" and "Register" links below it. After successful authentication, the login panel is replaced by the Account Services navigation menu, which lists clickable links for Open New Account, Accounts Overview, Transfer Funds, Bill Pay, Find Transactions, Update Contact Info, Request Loan, and Log Out. The "Accounts Overview" link is highlighted by default as the landing page. A top header bar displays the ParaBank logo on the left, and links for Home, About Us, and Contact Us on the right side. At the very top, a secondary navigation bar provides links to Solutions, About Us, and Admin Page. The main content area on the right side changes dynamically based on the selected Account Services menu item, displaying forms, tables, and confirmation messages relevant to each banking function. The footer at the bottom contains links for Home, About Us, Services, Products, Locations, and Admin Page along with copyright information.

Functional Overview.1. Login: The login page displays the ParaBank homepage with the ParaBank logo and tagline "Experience the difference" at the top. The left sidebar contains the Customer Login panel with a "Customer Login" heading, a Username text input field, a Password text input field (masked), and a blue "Log In" button. Directly beneath the button are two links: "Forgot login info?" which navigates to the customer lookup page, and "Register" which navigates to the registration form. The main content area displays promotional content about ParaBank services including ATM Services and Online Services sections with bullet-pointed feature lists. When the user enters valid credentials and clicks Log In, the system submits the credentials to the server for authentication. If the credentials match a registered account, the Customer Login panel is replaced by the Account Services navigation menu and the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview dashboard. If the credentials do not match or any error occurs during authentication, an error message "The username and password could not be verified." or "An internal error has occurred and has been logged." is displayed in the main content area, the username field retains the entered value, the password field is cleared, and the user can correct their entry and click Log In again. If either field is left empty, an error message "Please enter a username and password." is displayed.
Functional Overview.2. Forgot Password: The Forgot Login Info page is accessed by clicking the "Forgot login info?" link in the Customer Login panel. The left sidebar continues to display the Customer Login form with Username and Password fields, a Log In button, and the "Forgot login info?" and "Register" links. The main content area displays the heading "Customer Lookup" followed by a form with seven required text input fields arranged vertically: First Name, Last Name, Address, City, State, Zip Code, and SSN (Social Security Number). Each field has a corresponding label to the left. A "Find My Login Info" button appears below the form fields. If any field is left blank and the button is clicked, the page displays an error message prompting the user to complete all fields. Once all fields are filled and the "Find My Login Info" button is clicked, the system searches for a matching customer record in the database. If no matching record is found, the page displays the error message "The customer information could not be found." in the main content area. If a matching record is found, the page displays a success message with the heading "Your login information was located successfully. You are now logged in." and the user is automatically authenticated and redirected to the Accounts Overview page.
Functional Overview.3. Register: The Register page is accessed by clicking the "Register" link in the Customer Login panel. The left sidebar continues to display the Customer Login form with Username and Password fields, a Log In button, and the "Forgot login info?" and "Register" links. The main content area displays the heading "Signing up is easy!" followed by a registration form with two sections. The first section has an orange "Your information:" subheading and contains seven text input fields: First Name, Last Name, Address, City, State, Zip Code, and Phone # (all with labels to the left). The second section has an orange "Login information:" subheading and contains SSN, Username, Password, and Confirm fields. All eleven fields are mandatory and impose no additional format or content constraints beyond being non-empty. A "Register" button appears at the bottom of the form. When the user fills in all fields and clicks Register, the system validates that all fields are populated and that the Password and Confirm values match. If any required field is blank, the corresponding error message appears next to that field (e.g., "First name is required", "Last name is required"). If the Username already exists, an error "This username already exists." is displayed. Upon successful validation, the system creates the new customer account, automatically logs the user in, displays a welcome message "Your account was created successfully. You are now logged in." with the new username, and the Account Services menu replaces the Customer Login panel.
Functional Overview.4. Open New Account: The Open New Account page is accessed from the Account Services menu in the left sidebar, with "Open New Account" highlighted as the active selection. The main content area displays the heading "Open New Account" followed by a form. The form contains a "What type of Account?" label with a dropdown selector offering two options: "CHECKING" and "SAVINGS". Below that, a section reads "What account would you like to use for your new account's initial deposit? A minimum of $100.00 must be deposited into the new account at the time of creation." followed by a second dropdown listing all of the user's existing account numbers as funding source options. A blue "Open New Account" button appears below both dropdowns. When the user selects an account type, chooses a funding source account, and clicks the button, the system deducts $100.00 from the selected source account and creates a new account of the chosen type. Upon success, a confirmation page displays the heading "Account Opened!" with the message "Congratulations, your account is now open." and shows the new account number as a clickable link. Clicking the new account number navigates to the Account Activity page for that account. If the source account has insufficient funds or any server error occurs, an appropriate error message is displayed.
Functional Overview.5. Account Overview: The Accounts Overview page is the default landing page after login and is also accessible by clicking "Accounts Overview" in the Account Services menu on the left sidebar, which appears highlighted. The main content area displays the heading "Accounts Overview" above a table with three columns: Account, Balance, and Available Amount. Each row in the table represents one of the customer's accounts: the Account column displays the account number as a blue clickable link, the Balance column shows the current account balance in dollar format (e.g., "$1,034.50"), and the Available Amount column shows the available balance. Account numbers with negative balances display the amount in red text. At the bottom of the table, a "Total" row shows the combined total balance and total available amount across all accounts in bold. Clicking any account number link navigates to the Account Activity page for that account, which displays the Account Details (account number, type, balance, and available amount) and the Account Activity section with a table of recent transactions showing columns for Date, Transaction, Debit, and Credit. Each transaction link in the Activity table is clickable and leads to a Transaction Details page displaying the transaction ID, date, description, type (Debit or Credit), and amount.
Functional Overview.6. Transfer Funds: The Transfer Funds page is accessed from the Account Services menu in the left sidebar, with "Transfer Funds" highlighted. The main content area displays the heading "Transfer Funds" followed by a form with three components: an Amount text input field preceded by a "$" symbol for entering the dollar amount to transfer, a "From account #" dropdown listing all of the user's account numbers as the source account, and a "To account #" dropdown listing all of the user's account numbers as the destination account. A blue "Transfer" button appears below the form fields. When the user enters a valid dollar amount, selects both source and destination accounts, and clicks Transfer, the system processes the transaction by debiting the specified amount from the source account and crediting it to the destination account. Upon successful completion, the page displays a confirmation message with the heading "Transfer Complete!" showing "$[amount] has been transferred from account #[source] to account #[destination]." with both account numbers displayed as clickable links. If the amount field is empty or invalid, or if there are insufficient funds, an error message is displayed.
Functional Overview.7. Bill Payments: The Bill Pay page is accessed from the Account Services menu in the left sidebar, with "Bill Pay" highlighted. The main content area displays the heading "Bill Payment Service" followed by a form divided into two sections. The left section contains payee information fields: Payee Name (text input), Address (text input), City (text input), State (text input), Zip Code (text input), Phone # (text input), Account # (text input for the payee's account number), and Verify Account # (text input to confirm the payee's account number). The right section contains Amount (text input preceded by "$") and a "From account #" dropdown listing all of the user's account numbers. A blue "Send Payment" button appears at the bottom of the form. All fields are required; if any field is left blank, a validation message appears next to the empty field (e.g., "Payee name is required", "Address is required", "Account number is required"). If the two account number fields do not match, an error "The account numbers do not match" is displayed. When all fields are properly filled and the user clicks Send Payment, the system processes the payment by debiting the specified amount from the selected account. Upon success, a confirmation page displays the heading "Bill Payment Complete" with the message "Bill Payment to [Payee Name] in the amount of $[amount] from account [account number] was successful." and a "See Account Activity for more details" heading appears below.
Functional Overview.8. Find Transaction: The Find Transactions page is accessed from the Account Services menu in the left sidebar, with "Find Transactions" highlighted. The main content area displays the heading "Find Transactions" followed by an account selection dropdown labeled "Select Account" listing all of the user's account numbers. Below the dropdown, four distinct search panels are displayed vertically. The first panel, "Find by Transaction ID," contains a single text input field and a "Find Transactions" button. The second panel, "Find by Date," contains a single date text input field with the placeholder format "MM-DD-YYYY" and a "Find Transactions" button. The third panel, "Find by Date Range," contains two date text input fields labeled "between" and "and" with the same MM-DD-YYYY format and a "Find Transactions" button. The fourth panel, "Find by Amount," contains a single text input field preceded by "$" and a "Find Transactions" button. All input fields are required within their respective panels. If a user submits with empty or invalid input, an inline validation message appears next to the field (e.g., "Invalid date format" for incorrect date entries). When a valid search is submitted, the system queries the selected account's transaction history and returns matching results in a table below with columns for Date, Transaction (as a clickable link), Debit (amount debited), and Credit (amount credited). Clicking a transaction link navigates to the Transaction Details page. If no matching transactions are found, the message "No transactions found" is displayed.
Functional Overview.9. Update Profile: The Update Contact Info page is accessed from the Account Services menu in the left sidebar, with "Update Contact Info" highlighted. The main content area displays the heading "Update Profile" above a form containing seven text input fields arranged vertically with labels: First Name, Last Name, Address, City, State, Zip Code, and Phone #. Each field is pre-populated with the user's current information stored in the database. All fields are required; if any field is left blank, a validation message appears next to that field (e.g., "First name is required"). A blue "Update Profile" button appears at the bottom of the form. When the user modifies any fields and clicks Update Profile, the system validates all inputs and updates the customer record in the database. Upon successful submission, a confirmation message "Your updated address and phone number have been added to the system." is displayed in the main content area below the heading "Profile Updated." The updated information is reflected immediately across the application.
Functional Overview.10. Request Loan: The Request Loan page is accessed from the Account Services menu in the left sidebar, with "Request Loan" highlighted. The main content area displays the heading "Apply for a Loan" above a form with three fields: Loan Amount (text input preceded by "$"), Down Payment (text input preceded by "$"), and a "From Account #" dropdown listing all of the user's account numbers to cover the down payment. A blue "Apply Now" button appears below the form fields. All fields are required; if any field is left blank, validation error messages appear. When the user enters the loan amount, down payment, selects a source account, and clicks Apply Now, the system processes the loan application by evaluating the request against lending criteria. If approved, a confirmation page displays the heading "Loan Request Processed" with a table showing the Loan Provider Name, a "Status: Approved" message in green text, and a "New account number:" field showing the newly created loan account number as a clickable blue link. The new account appears in the Accounts Overview. If the loan is denied (e.g., insufficient down payment relative to loan amount), the status shows "Denied" with an explanation message. If any server error occurs, the message "We cannot process your loan application at this time. Please try again later." is displayed.
Functional Overview.Logout: The logout function is accessed by clicking "Log Out" in the Account Services menu on the left sidebar. When clicked, the system immediately ends the user's authenticated session, clears any session-related data from the server, and redirects the user to the ParaBank homepage displaying the Customer Login panel with empty Username and Password fields. The Account Services menu is no longer visible, confirming the session has been terminated. This ensures that no account information, transaction data, or personal details remain accessible after logout. If the user attempts to navigate to any authenticated page after logging out, they are redirected to the login page.
